metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `rebar fsck --fix` with `rebar fsck --repair` everywhere in the plan and in the resulting `docs/migrations.md` (verified against `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt` and `fsck_cli` in `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py`).
findings.finding: The plan names `rebar fsck --fix` as one of the three reconciliation entry points that future authors should document and users should run, but the actual mutating flag on the `fsck` command is `--repair` (not `--fix`). The `--fix` flag belongs to `rebar validate`, a separate command. Propagating the wrong flag name into `docs/migrations.md` would produce actively misleading documentation.
findings.location: What section: 'init/re-init, `rebar fsck --fix`, MCP server startup — all reconcile unconditionally under the write lock'; AC2: 'Documents the entry points'
findings.suggested_fix: Change every occurrence of `rebar fsck --fix` in the plan and the doc to `rebar fsck --repair`. The correct invocation to drive idempotent store repair is `rebar fsck --repair` (optionally with `--dry-run` or `--limit=N`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace every occurrence of `rebar fsck --fix` in the plan (What section and AC2) with `rebar fsck --repair`, matching the actual flag in `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt` and `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py`.
findings.finding: The plan names 'rebar fsck --fix' as one of the three entry points the documentation will describe as reconciling unconditionally, but '--fix' does not exist in the codebase. The actual mutating flag is '--repair' (confirmed by src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt line 1: 'Usage: rebar fsck [--output json] [--repair [--dry-run] [--limit=N]]' and src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py line 682: 'do_repair = "--repair" in argv'). Writing '--fix' into a first-class guide will document a dead command that will fail when users run it.
findings.location: What section: 'the entry points (init/re-init, `rebar fsck --fix`, MCP server startup)' and AC line 2: 'Documents the entry points'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'rebar fsck --fix' → 'rebar fsck --repair'. In AC2, update the entry-point description to use '--repair'. Verify against src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt before finalizing the doc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct 'rebar fsck --fix' to 'rebar fsck --repair' throughout docs/migrations.md (the mutating flag on fsck is '--repair'; '--fix' belongs to 'rebar validate').
findings.finding: The plan's What section specifies that docs/migrations.md should document 'rebar fsck --fix' as an entry point, but the actual mutating flag on rebar fsck is '--repair' (not '--fix'). '--fix' is the flag for 'rebar validate'. An implementer following the plan as written will produce a doc with a wrong command that causes an unknown-flag error when a future agent or human runs it.
findings.location: What section: 'the entry points (init/re-init, `rebar fsck --fix`, MCP server startup — all reconcile unconditionally under the write lock)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace every occurrence of 'rebar fsck --fix' in the plan's What section (and the resulting doc) with 'rebar fsck --repair', which is the flag that drives the store to fsck-zero (confirmed in src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt and src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `rebar fsck --fix` with `rebar fsck --repair` throughout the plan's What section and all ACs, matching the verified CLI flag in `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt`.
findings.finding: The plan names 'rebar fsck --fix' as an entry point for the ensure reconciliation path, but the actual CLI flag is '--repair' (verified from src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt line 1: 'Usage: rebar fsck [--output json] [--repair [--dry-run] [--limit=N]]'). There is no '--fix' flag. If the doc is written with '--fix', it will be incorrect and mislead users and agents who try to run the command.
findings.location: Plan: 'What' section — 'the entry points (init/re-init, `rebar fsck --fix`, MCP server startup'; also Acceptance Criteria AC2: 'Documents the entry points, `.ensure-applied` + `.ensure-hinted`, the pending-hint, and the marker-gates-hint-never-repair invariant'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section and AC2, replace every instance of `rebar fsck --fix` with `rebar fsck --repair` to match the actual flag shown in `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `rebar fsck --fix` with `rebar fsck --repair` throughout the plan (What section and AC2), matching the actual flag in `src/rebar/_cli/help/fsck.txt`.
findings.finding: The plan instructs documenting 'rebar fsck --fix' as an entry point, but the actual CLI flag in the codebase is '--repair' (not '--fix'). This is a tool-verified pink-elephant antipattern: the doc will encode and propagate the wrong command string.
findings.location: What section: 'the entry points (init/re-init, `rebar fsck --fix`, MCP server startup)'; AC2: 'Documents the entry points'
findings.suggested_fix: Change all occurrences of `rebar fsck --fix` to `rebar fsck --repair` in the plan text and the doc it will produce.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact file path of the 'docs index' in the plan (e.g. `docs/README.md`) so the executor does not have to guess.
findings.finding: The 'docs index' file that must receive a link is referenced by role only, not by path. An executor must guess whether it is docs/README.md, docs/index.md, or another file, creating a stop-and-ask moment.
findings.location: Scope section ('In: docs/migrations.md + the index/CLAUDE.md link') and AC5
findings.suggested_fix: In Scope and AC5, replace 'the docs index' with the concrete path, e.g. 'docs/README.md'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact file that serves as the 'docs index' (e.g. `docs/README.md` or `docs/index.md`) in both the What section and the verifiable AC so implementers and reviewers target the same file.
findings.finding: The 'docs index' file that must receive the backlink is never named in the plan or its ACs — implementers don't know which file to edit, and verifiers don't know which file to check for the link string.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet: 'A link to docs/migrations.md is added from the docs index and CLAUDE.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the docs index' with the concrete filename, e.g.: 'A link to `docs/migrations.md` is added from `docs/README.md` and `CLAUDE.md` (verifiable: the markdown link `[migrations](migrations.md)` or similar is present in both files).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an anchored proving command to AC5, e.g. `grep -E '\[.*\]\(.*docs/migrations\.md\)' docs/README.md CLAUDE.md` to confirm a Markdown link (not just a mention) is present in both files.
findings.finding: AC5 asserts 'the link string is present in both' but supplies no anchored proving command, leaving the verification open to substring false-positives or executor interpretation of what constitutes a valid link.
findings.location: AC5: 'A link to docs/migrations.md is added from the docs index and CLAUDE.md (verifiable: the link string is present in both)'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC5: 'Proving command: `grep -rE "\[.*\]\(.*docs/migrations\.md\)" docs/README.md CLAUDE.md` — both files must match.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cross-reference in the plan (or AC1) to the WS1 plan's definition of the ok/changed/failed result type, so the doc author writes from the authoritative type spec rather than inventing it.
findings.finding: The plan asks docs/migrations.md to document a 'check-then-act callable returning ok/changed/failed' as a 'typed result', but neither the enum/type vocabulary nor its container type (e.g. EnsureResult dataclass, Literal, NamedTuple) exists anywhere in the codebase. The doc author has no grounded specification to write from.
findings.location: What section: 'a check-then-act callable returning ok/changed/failed'; AC1: 'typed result'
